assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 20-24: 'TC-8006 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.1] (stream 2.1.x)', 'TC-8001 stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x)', 'Classification: **Different-stream sibling** (companion tracker)', 'TC-8001 is NOT a same-stream duplicate. It is a cross-stream companion issue created by PSIRT to track the same CVE in a different product version stream.'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 32-46: 'Per Step 4.2, before creating a "Related" link, check the current issue's existing issuelinks array for a matching link.' The Pre-existing Link Check section explicitly inspects the issuelinks array fetched in Step 1, checking type.name=='Related' and outwardIssue.key=='TC-8001', with link ID 1990401.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 47-52: 'Result: A matching Related link to TC-8001 already exists.' 'Action: **Skip link creation.**' 'No jira.create_link call was made. This is the correct idempotent behavior -- re-running triage on an issue that already has its sibling links in place does not create duplicate links.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 50: '"Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping"'. Also repeated in triage-outcome.md line 30 with the same log message.

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 63-69: A 'Sibling Landscape' table is presented with columns Issue/Stream/Status/Affects Versions, showing both TC-8001 (stream 2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (current, stream 2.1.x, New). This table appears after the link-skip decision, confirming the sibling summary is independent of link creation.
